title: Count Angles/Vertices of a Polygon
description: Determine the number of angles (vertices) in a 2D polygon by counting its corner points.
response: Count and state the total number of angles (vertices).
label: Production / Skill
example: A polygon with 6 corners has 6 angles.

title: Count Sides of a Polygon
description: Determine the number of sides of a 2D polygon by counting its line segments.
response: Count and state the total number of sides.
label: Production / Skill
example: A shape with 5 line segments has 5 sides.

title: Determine a.m. or p.m. from Context
description: Use contextual clues (e.g., daytime school, playing soccer) to decide whether a given time is a.m. or p.m.
condition: A time is given with a real-world scene or context indicating time of day.
response: Select either a.m. or p.m. consistent with the context.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the time is 8:40 and the picture shows children arriving at school in the morning, choose **a.m.**.

title: Identify Attributes of a Cube
description: Recall the number of faces, edges, and vertices of a cube.
response: State that it has 6 faces, 12 edges, and 8 vertices.
label: Fact
example: A cube has 6 faces, 12 edges, and 8 vertices.

title: More Equal Shares Means Smaller Pieces
description: Recognize that dividing a whole into more equal parts results in smaller individual shares.
condition: Compare equal partitions of the same whole with different numbers of shares.
response: Conclude that the partition with more shares has smaller pieces.
label: Concept
example: A brownie cut into 4 equal pieces has smaller shares than one cut into 2 equal pieces.

title: Name Polygon by Number of Sides
description: Identify and name a polygon based on its number of sides.
condition: Given a polygon with n sides (e.g., 4, 5, 6).
response: State the corresponding polygon name (e.g., quadrilateral, pentagon, hexagon).
label: Rule / Plan
example: If a polygon has 6 sides, it is a **hexagon**.

title: Read Time on an Analog Clock
description: Determine the hour and minute shown by the positions of the hour and minute hands.
condition: Given an analog clock with hour and minute hands.
response: State the time in hours and minutes (e.g., 2:55, 6:10).
label: Production / Skill
example: If the minute hand points to 11 (55 minutes) and the hour hand is near 3, the time is $2{:}55$.

title: Skip Count by Fives
description: Count forward by increments of five starting from zero.
condition: Given a number line starting at 0 with instruction to count by fives.
label: Principle / Model
